name: Chat: presence — "Active now" in thread header
desc:
The last remaining 1:1 gap vs the ToyRent chat design: the thread header shows the counterpart's name but no "Active now" presence line.

Scope: a real presence service (SignalR connection tracking, last-seen), presence exposed on ChatConversationDetailsResponse, rendered in the thread header.

Do NOT fake it with a static label — the design implies live presence.

Unblocked: M-008 (docker nginx hub proxy) is FIXED as of 2026-07-11, so SignalR now works in the docker stack too.

Source: orchestrator gap analysis, 2026-07-11.

name: Chat: push notifications for new messages

No push/notification when a new chat message arrives while the app is closed or the user is not on the chat tab.

Realtime (SignalR) delivers in-app only. Consider reusing the existing Notification vertical (INotificationEmitter) and/or web push.

Source: knowledge/feature-notes/2026-07-chat-phase1.md — Deferred.

name: Centralized API error handling & localization
desc:
Today every feature's NgRx effects pass HTTP errors straight to `toApiErrorMessage` (Rental-Ui/src/app/api/http-error-message.util.ts), which renders the server's `detail`/`title` VERBATIM. Two concrete consequences, both observed live by the verifier during the chat image-attachments work (2026-07-11):

1. On a 500, a full C# stack trace — including local filesystem paths — was rendered in the user-facing error banner.
2. Server-side rejections show hardcoded English. A text file renamed .jpg passes the browser's extension-derived File.type check, so the SERVER's magic-byte rejection is the one the user actually sees — and a Russian/Armenian user got English.

The chat feature now maps ServiceError codes to i18n keys, but ONLY locally in `features/chat/store/chat.effects.ts`. There is NO app-wide precedent: no other feature reads the server's error code at all. That local map is the seed for this task, not the solution.

Goal: one ErrorMapper for the whole app.
- map `error.code` → i18n key
- sanitize `detail` before it ever reaches the user
- never display internal exceptions or stack traces
- one consistent error banner/dialog surface
- a safe fallback for unknown/unmapped errors

RESOLVED since this card was written (2026-07-12): the ServiceError code no longer lives in the ProblemDetails `type` member. It moved to its own top-level **`errorCode`** member, `type` is now a `urn:rental:error:{code}` URI, and all 8 controllers go through one shared helper. The frontend contract layer models the envelope in `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/api-error.model.ts` (`ApiProblemDetails`, `ApiErrorCode` with the 52 real codes, and `getApiErrorCode()` — the single safe reader). USE `getApiErrorCode`; do not re-derive extraction, and never parse the `type` URN.

Source: verifier findings, chat image-attachments vertical (2026-07-11). See knowledge/mistakes.md M-013.
prompt:
Build centralized API error handling and localization in Rental-Ui (work on branch `dev`; do not commit, do not merge to main).

CONTEXT — read first:
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/api-error.model.ts` — the contract layer, ALREADY BUILT. `ApiProblemDetails` models the error envelope; `ApiErrorCode` enumerates the 52 real ServiceError codes; `getApiErrorCode()` is the single safe reader (the code lives in the top-level `errorCode` member; `type` is an opaque `urn:rental:error:{code}` URI — NEVER parse it). Use these; do not re-derive them.
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/api/http-error-message.util.ts` — today's behaviour and the core defect: renders the server's `detail`/`title` VERBATIM for any unmapped error. On a 500 this leaked a full C# stack trace with local filesystem paths into the user-facing banner (observed live by the verifier).
- `Rental-Ui/src/app/features/chat/store/chat.effects.ts` — the ONLY existing code→i18n mapping (`CHAT_ERROR_MESSAGE_KEYS`, 3 codes). Deliberately chat-local. This is your seed, not your solution.
- Every other feature's effects (auth, bookings, listings, reviews, admin, favorites, profile, notifications, public-profiles) is a bare passthrough — audit them all.

BUILD:
1. A shared ErrorMapper in `src/app/api/` — the single entry point every feature's effects use. It resolves the code via `getApiErrorCode`, maps it to an i18n key, and returns a translated message.
2. SANITIZE: `detail` must never reach the user raw. An unmapped error falls back to a safe, translated generic message, never to server-supplied prose, and NEVER to an exception message or stack trace. Treat this as a hard security rule, not a nicety.
3. Migrate every feature's effects to the shared mapper; delete the per-feature passthroughs and fold the chat-local map into the shared one.
4. One consistent error surface (banner/dialog) instead of each feature inventing its own.
5. i18n keys in en/ru/hy for every mapped code and for the fallback.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT change backend files.
- Codes come from `ApiErrorCode` / the backend's `ErrorCodes` constants — never invent code strings.

DONE WHEN: `npm run build` clean, `npm test` green, plus specs proving (a) a mapped code renders its translated message in a non-English locale, (b) an unmapped 500 whose `detail` contains a stack trace renders ONLY the generic fallback and leaks nothing. Report which features you migrated and any codes you could not map.

name: M-005: harden EF MigrateAsync against SQL-startup race (docker)
desc:
OPEN follow-up. The docker API crashes (exit 139) on restart: the db healthcheck (SELECT 1 on master) passes before the user DB is online, so EF Core Database.MigrateAsync() sees "does not exist", issues CREATE DATABASE, and hits SqlException 1801 (already exists) — unhandled, process dies.

Current workaround is operational: docker compose -f rental-api/docker-compose.yml up -d api (succeeds on retry).

Proper fix: retry loop / EnableRetryOnFailure in MigrationExtensions.ApplyMigrationsAsync so transient SQL-startup errors are retried instead of crashing.

Source: knowledge/mistakes.md — M-005 (still OPEN).

name: Backups: off-site copy of production backups
desc:
Daily backups (.bak + uploads tgz, cron 03:30, 7-day retention) land ONLY in /opt/dorent/backups on the same VPS — a single disk failure or Hetzner account loss takes both the database and its backups. Decision needed from Tigran first: Backblaze B2 free tier (10 GB, via rclone) vs periodic pull to his PC.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13; DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md backup section marks off-site as pending; ADR-003 amendment.
prompt: Prerequisite from Tigran: a Backblaze B2 account + application key (or an explicit choice of the pull-to-PC variant). Then, on the dorent.am server (SSH): install rclone, configure a B2 remote (credentials live only on the server, never in the repo), extend deploy/backup-production.sh (rental-api repo, branch dev) with an rclone copy of /opt/dorent/backups after a successful local backup, add remote-side retention (e.g. 30 days), update the backup section of DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md, and prove it: run the cron entry once manually, then restore a .bak downloaded FROM B2 via ./deploy/backup-production.sh --verify semantics. Commit script/doc changes on dev, do not push to main.

name: Monitoring: UptimeRobot for dorent.am
desc:
No uptime monitoring: if the site goes down, nobody is notified. Human task (needs a personal account, no agent access): sign up at uptimerobot.com (free plan), add HTTP(s) monitor for https://dorent.am/ (5-min interval, email alert), plus a Keyword monitor on https://dorent.am/api/listings?page=1&pageSize=1 (keyword: items) to catch the nginx-alive-but-API-dead case.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13, stage 7.

name: Security: change bootstrap admin password + rotate Cloudflare tunnel token
desc:
Two hygiene items from the 2026-07-13 production deploy:

1. The bootstrap admin password (login tigransargsyan062@gmail.com) was machine-generated at deploy time and is stored outside the server. Log in at https://dorent.am and change it via profile — after that the stored copy becomes a dead artifact.

2. The Cloudflare tunnel token transited the chat once during setup. Rotate it: Cloudflare Zero Trust → Networks → Tunnels → dorent-production → Refresh token, put the new value into /opt/dorent/rental-api/.env (CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN), then docker compose -f docker-compose.production.yml up -d cloudflared.

Mostly human steps; an agent can do item 2 over SSH once the new token is provided in .env.

name: DNS: clean up legacy cPanel records in Cloudflare zone dorent.am
desc:
The zone still carries the dead Name.am cPanel legacy, all pointing at the suspended host 161.97.154.98: A-records cpanel/cpcalendars/cpcontacts/ftp/webdisk/webmail/whm, CNAME mail → dorent.am, a Cloudflare placeholder MX (_dc-mx.…), SPF TXT referencing the dead IP, and cPanel SRV/DKIM/ACME/DCV records. None of them serve anything.

Constraints agreed during the deploy: mail @dorent.am is unused today but may be wanted later; nothing gets deleted without Tigran's explicit per-record approval. The live records dorent.am + www.dorent.am (type Tunnel → dorent-production) must not be touched.
prompt: Ask Tigran for a fresh screenshot/export of Cloudflare DNS → Records for dorent.am. Produce a two-column table: KEEP (the two Tunnel records, and anything Tigran wants for future mail) vs DELETE (every record pointing at 161.97.154.98 or cPanel services: A cpanel/cpcalendars/cpcontacts/ftp/webdisk/webmail/whm, CNAME mail, placeholder MX _dc-mx.*, SPF TXT with ip4:161.97.154.98, SRV _autodiscover/_caldav*/_carddav*, cPanel DKIM/ACME/DCV TXT). Present the table, get explicit approval, then walk Tigran through deleting (or delete via API if credentials are provided). Note in the table: when real mail is wanted later, proper MX/SPF/DKIM for the chosen provider (e.g. Zoho/Google) replace the legacy ones — the tunnel records are unrelated.

name: Auth: register https://dorent.am in Google OAuth origins
desc:
Google Sign-In on production will fail until https://dorent.am (and https://www.dorent.am) are added to Authorized JavaScript origins of the OAuth client ending …oupt.apps.googleusercontent.com in Google Cloud Console. Human step (console access); afterwards verify the Google login flow live on https://dorent.am.

Source: production deploy session 2026-07-13, open item.

name: Infra: rescale VPS to 4 GB (CPX21) when budget allows
desc:
The 2 GB CPX12 + 4 GB swap + MSSQL_MEMORY_LIMIT_MB=1024 setup is a deliberate temporary compromise (ADR-003, amended 2026-07-13) — under real traffic SQL Server will start swapping and latency will degrade.

Upgrade path with minimal risk: Hetzner console → server → Rescale → CPX21 (3 vCPU / 4 GB) with "CPU and RAM only" (keeps the 40 GB disk, so the change stays reversible), ~2 min downtime. Afterwards raise or drop MSSQL_MEMORY_LIMIT_MB in docker-compose.production.yml (rental-api dev branch) and consider removing the 4 GB swap dependency note in DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md.

name: QA: accessibility automation baseline
desc:
Deliberately deferred out of the qa-engineer agent scope (2026-07-14): a11y automation without a baseline just automates noise. Prerequisite: a one-time manual a11y audit of the critical pages (axe DevTools or similar) to establish the baseline; then decide what is worth automating (axe-core in the mocked Playwright tier is the likely cheapest layer).

Source: qa-engineer design discussion, 2026-07-14.

name: QA: cross-browser matrix for critical journeys
desc:
Deliberately deferred out of the qa-engineer agent scope (2026-07-14): Playwright currently installs chromium only. After the real-stack booking journey suite stabilizes, evaluate adding firefox/webkit projects for the mocked (fast) tier only — real-stack stays single-browser to keep runtime sane.

Source: qa-engineer design discussion, 2026-07-14.

name: Infra: stop merging console output into backup.log (cron redirect)
desc:
Design smell behind M-015: the crontab entry appends the backup script's stdout+stderr into the same file the script writes structured entries to:

  30 3 * * * /opt/dorent/rental-api/deploy/backup-production.sh >> /opt/dorent/backups/backup.log 2>&1

That interleaving is what made smoke.sh grade prose instead of the outcome (fixed defensively in rental-api 33618a5, but the smell remains). Cleaner end state: drop the >> redirect entirely (the script already logs its own outcomes and its ERR trap records failures), or send console output to a separate /opt/dorent/backups/backup.out.

Server-side change (crontab) + DEPLOY-PRODUCTION.md update. Task for platform-engineer.

Source: knowledge/mistakes.md M-015, 2026-07-14.

name: Repo hygiene: .gitattributes eol=lf for deploy/*.sh + fix stale git remote URL
desc:
Two small papercuts found during the 2026-07-14 production work (platform-engineer):

1. deploy/*.sh are stored LF in the git index but check out CRLF on Windows. Harmless for git, but a script scp'd straight from a Windows working tree to Linux gets a broken shebang (env: 'bash'). Fix: add .gitattributes with `*.sh text eol=lf` in rental-api (and consider the same for Rental-Ui).

2. `git push` in rental-api prints "remote: This repository moved" — origin still points at github.com/Tigran-developer/rental-api while the repo now lives at TTigSSar/rental-api. GitHub redirects today, but redirects are not forever. Fix: `git remote set-url origin https://github.com/TTigSSar/rental-api.git` (same check for Rental-Ui).
